Bill Ntwali

Columbia undergraduate at the intersection of software engineering, computational biology, and data-driven problem solving. I build tools that connect technical depth with real-world usefulness.

About Me

The person behind the builds

Builder

I think in systems and ship real products — from nonprofit platforms to browser extensions. If it solves a problem, I want to build it.

Research-Minded

Computational biology sits at an extraordinary intersection of data, biology, and computing. I'm drawn to scientific problems where code becomes discovery.

Data-Focused

Good decisions come from good data. I love turning raw information into insight — whether through analysis, visualization, or building the pipelines that make it possible.

Community-Driven

As a Resident Adviser and mentor, I understand that technical work doesn't happen in isolation. People, trust, and support structures matter just as much as code.

I'm a Columbia University undergraduate studying at the intersection of biology and computing — a space where algorithms illuminate biological systems and data reshapes how we understand living things. My academic path has been shaped by a genuine curiosity: not just what the tools do, but why they work and how they can be made more useful.

That curiosity extends beyond coursework. I've built full-stack applications, dug into data pipelines, and shipped tools that real people use. I believe in learning by building, and building toward things that actually matter.

Computer Science & Biology

Experience

Where I've shown up

Resident Adviser

Columbia University Housing

Aug 2024 – PresentLeadership

Serve as a live-in community leader for a floor of Columbia undergraduates, providing support, programming, and crisis navigation. This role has sharpened my ability to lead with empathy, handle ambiguity, and build trust at scale.

  • On-call crisis support and student wellness coordination
  • Designed and facilitated community programming and academic workshops
  • Collaborated with Dean of Students office on policy and student affairs

Research Assistant

Computational Biology Lab

Jan 2024 – PresentResearch

Contribute to ongoing research at the intersection of computational methods and biological systems. Work involves data processing pipelines, genomic analysis, and supporting research workflows with software tooling.

  • Built data cleaning and analysis pipelines for genomic datasets
  • Contributed to literature reviews and experimental design discussions
  • Developed reproducible research workflows using Python and R

Software Engineering Intern

Tech Organization

Summer 2024Engineering

Contributed to full-stack feature development and analytics tooling in a fast-moving engineering team. Worked across frontend and backend systems and participated in code review, sprint planning, and technical design discussions.

  • Built and shipped user-facing features in React and TypeScript
  • Improved data pipeline performance with backend optimization
  • Participated in sprint reviews, retros, and architectural discussions

Academic Tutor & Mentor

Columbia Academic Programs

Sep 2023 – PresentMentorship

Provide academic support in STEM subjects to Columbia undergraduates, with a focus on making technical material accessible and building genuine understanding rather than surface-level recall.

  • One-on-one tutoring in CS, math, and biology coursework
  • Developed supplemental study materials for complex topics
  • Helped students navigate academic challenges and build confidence

Hackathon Participant & Builder

Multiple Hackathons

2023 – 2025Building

Competed in multiple hackathons, shipping functional prototypes under time pressure. Strong emphasis on product thinking, real-world relevance, and clean execution in constrained environments.

  • Shipped MVPs across web, data, and healthcare-adjacent domains
  • Led or contributed to cross-functional teams under 24–48 hour timelines
  • Pitched technical products to judges and stakeholder audiences

Exploring

What keeps me curious

The domains and problems I find myself returning to, reading about, and wanting to build in.

Software Engineering

Clean systems, thoughtful architecture, and the craft of building software that lasts.

Computational Biology

Algorithms that reveal patterns in living systems — where code meets the biology of life.

Bioinformatics

Building tools and pipelines that help researchers navigate genomic and proteomic data.

Data-Driven Products

Making data meaningful — through visualization, analysis, and systems that help people decide.

Developer Tools

Extensions, CLIs, and platforms that make other builders faster and more capable.

Healthcare & Nonprofit Tech

Technology at its most meaningful — software that serves people in high-stakes, high-need contexts.

Applied Research Systems

Software infrastructure that makes research faster, more reproducible, and more shareable.

AI & Intelligent Systems

Curious about how ML and AI can be embedded into useful, real-world products — especially in science.

Currently exploring

The intersection of machine learning and genomic data — specifically how AI-assisted tools can make bioinformatics workflows faster and more accessible to researchers.

Let's build something meaningful

I'm always excited to connect about software, data, research, and impact-driven work. Whether it's a role, a collaboration, or just a conversation — reach out.
